reforms.description: Australia's public spending on energy research, development and demonstration is significantly below the IEA average, prompting a recommendation to scale up and refocus public funding towards clean energy and energy-efficiency technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for clean energy technologies is expected to enhance innovation and drive economic growth through the transition to renewable energy.
reforms.status_evidence: Consider scaling up and refocusing public funding towards the development and demonstration of clean energy and energy-efficiency technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public investment strategy towards clean energy, which is crucial for meeting climate targets and fostering sustainable economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Australia’s public spending on energy research, development and demonstration is significantly below the IEA average.

reforms.description: The government is encouraged to further increase support for agricultural research and development, focusing on emissions reduction technologies and practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing agricultural R&D is expected to improve productivity and sustainability in the sector, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Further increase support for agricultural research and development as well as extension services and agricultural education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to significantly enhance the agricultural sector's capacity to innovate and adapt to climate challenges, which is vital for food security and economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: Further increase support for agricultural research and development as well as extension services and agricultural education.

reforms.description: Australia plans to scale up and refocus public funding towards the development and demonstration of clean energy and energy-efficiency technologies to support the transition to net zero.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for clean energy technologies is expected to enhance innovation in the energy sector, leading to improved efficiency and reduced emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to scale up public funding for clean energy technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment aimed at fostering innovation in clean energy, which is crucial for meeting climate targets.
reforms.source_quote: Consider scaling up and refocusing public funding towards the development and demonstration of clean energy and energy-efficiency technologies.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced its intention to restrict concessional tax rates on superannuation earnings for individuals with balances exceeding AUD3 million, increasing the tax rate from 15% to 30%. This reform aims to address fiscal sustainability and equity in the tax system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing tax rates on high superannuation balances, the reform aims to enhance fiscal sustainability and reduce inequities in the tax system, potentially leading to more efficient allocation of resources.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced its intention to restrict the concessional tax rates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the tax treatment of superannuation, targeting high-income earners and addressing fiscal sustainability, which has broad implications for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have recently announced their intention to restrict the concessional tax rates by increasing the headline tax rate from 15% to 30% on earnings in superannuation accounts with balances exceeding AUD3 million.

reforms.description: The Australian government has raised the maximum amount of the childcare subsidy from 85% to 90% and reduced the speed at which the subsidy is withdrawn as family incomes increase, aiming to make childcare more affordable and accessible.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing childcare subsidies can enhance workforce participation, particularly among second earners, thereby boosting overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The maximum amount of the childcare subsidy has been officially raised and the withdrawal rate adjusted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing childcare support mechanisms, aimed at improving access and affordability, which is crucial for enhancing labor market participation.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal government has raised the maximum amount of the childcare subsidy from 85% to 90% and reduced the speed at which the subsidy is withdrawn as family incomes increase.

reforms.description: In July 2023, the Australian government combined two public parental leave payments into a single scheme, allowing partners to claim up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave, with plans to increase this to 26 weeks by 2026.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing parental leave policies can improve labor market outcomes for women, thereby increasing overall workforce participation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The two public payments were officially combined and new provisions for parental leave were announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the structure of parental leave in Australia, promoting gender equality in caregiving and supporting labor market re-entry for parents.
reforms.source_quote: In July 2023, the two public payments were combined, meaning that partners can now claim up to 20 weeks paid parental leave between them.

reforms.description: The Australian government is undertaking a 'Diversity in STEM' review to make recommendations on how to support women and other underrepresented groups in STEM education and careers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing participation of women in STEM fields can lead to a more skilled workforce, driving innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently undertaking a 'Diversity in STEM' review.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a significant gap in gender representation in STEM, which is crucial for future economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently undertaking a 'Diversity in STEM' review that will make recommendations on how the government can support women, and other underrepresented groups, to participate in STEM education, careers and industries.

reforms.description: The Federal government has introduced new legislation requiring firms with more than 100 employees to publicly report on gender pay gaps and other gender-related indicators.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing pay transparency can help reduce gender pay gaps, leading to better economic outcomes for women and overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Federal government prohibited pay secrecy clauses and introduced new legislation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how gender pay disparities are addressed in the workplace, with potential long-term impacts on gender equality.
reforms.source_quote: In 2022, the Federal government prohibited pay secrecy clauses in employment contracts and introduced new legislation that requires firms with more than 100 employees to publicly report on a range of areas including gender pay gaps.

reforms.description: The Safeguard Mechanism was reformed to align with Australia's revised emissions reduction targets, introducing below-baseline crediting and resetting baselines to encourage greater emissions reductions from industrial facilities.
reforms.package_name: 2023 Climate Change and Emissions Reduction Strategy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform incentivizes emissions reductions, potentially fostering innovation in low-carbon technologies and practices within the industrial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms became effective in July 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the existing emissions regulation framework, aiming to achieve ambitious national emissions reduction targets and drive innovation in the industrial sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Safeguard Mechanism (Crediting) Amendment Bill 2023 enacted a number of reforms to the Safeguard Mechanism, which have been in place since 1 July 2023.

reforms.description: The Australian government has committed around AUD 23 billion towards transforming the country’s energy system, including AUD 20 billion for the Rewiring the Nation programme to increase renewable energy capacity and invest in key transmission projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for renewable energy projects is expected to enhance energy capacity and support the transition to a cleaner energy system, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed around AUD 23 billion towards transforming the country’s energy system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the energy sector, aiming to transform Australia's energy landscape and support the transition to renewable energy, which is crucial for long-term economic sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The Australian Government has committed around AUD 23 billion (1% of GDP) until the end of 2025 towards transforming the country’s energy system.

reforms.description: The government has announced a Capacity Investment Scheme, a guarantee scheme to support the development of renewable generation and storage, expected to unlock AUD 10 billion in investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing guarantees for renewable projects, the scheme is likely to stimulate private investment, thereby enhancing the renewable energy sector and contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government also recently announced a Capacity Investment Scheme, a guarantee scheme to support the development of renewable generation and storage.
reforms.importance_rationale: This scheme is a significant step towards enhancing renewable energy capacity, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government also recently announced a Capacity Investment Scheme, a guarantee scheme to support the development of renewable generation and storage which is expected to unlock AUD 10 billion in investment.

reforms.description: The Australian government has committed up to AUD 3 billion from the National Reconstruction Fund to support investment in low emissions technologies and component manufacturing, alongside agricultural methane reduction initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to enhance technological advancements in agriculture, leading to increased productivity and reduced emissions, which can positively impact economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the commitment of up to AUD 3 billion for these initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant financial commitment to advancing low emissions technologies, which is crucial for addressing climate change and enhancing agricultural productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced the commitment of up to AUD 3 billion from the AUD 15 billion National Reconstruction Fund to support investment in low emissions technologies and component manufacturing and agricultural methane reduction.

reforms.description: The second and third stages of the Methane Emissions Reduction in Livestock (MERiL) Program will provide a total of AUD 20 million in funding to develop technologies for low emission feed supplements.
reforms.package_name: Methane Emissions Reduction in Livestock Program
reforms.component_name: Funding for Low Emission Feed Supplements
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding aims to develop innovative solutions that can significantly reduce methane emissions from livestock, thereby improving environmental sustainability and agricultural productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The second and third stages of the MERiL Program will provide funding to develop technologies for low emission feed supplements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a targeted investment in agricultural innovation that addresses specific environmental challenges, contributing to broader sustainability goals.
reforms.source_quote: The second and third stages of the Methane Emissions Reduction in Livestock (MERiL) Program will provide a total of AUD 20 million in funding to develop technologies to deliver low emission feed supplements.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced a AUD 1.3 billion Household Energy Upgrades Fund to support energy efficiency upgrades for households, including low-cost loans and funding for social housing improvements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to improve energy efficiency, which can lead to lower energy costs and increased household disposable income, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funds for the Household Energy Upgrades Fund in the 2023-24 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant investment in energy efficiency, which is crucial for meeting emission targets and improving household energy performance.
reforms.source_quote: In its 2023-24 Budget, the government announced it would provide AUD 1.3 billion to the Household Energy Upgrades Fund.

reforms.description: The federal government committed AUD 105 million to the New Energy Apprenticeships and New Energy Skills programs to deliver 10,000 energy apprentices and tailor skills training for new energy industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By training a skilled workforce for the renewable energy sector, the initiative supports the transition to a low-carbon economy and enhances job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funds for the New Energy Apprenticeships and Skills programs in the 2023-24 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding is significant for addressing skills shortages in the renewable energy sector, which is essential for achieving Australia's climate goals.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government committed AUD 105 million to the New Energy Apprenticeships and New Energy Skills programs.

reforms.description: The On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme was launched to provide AUD 100 million in funding for farmers to improve drought preparedness through the installation of on-farm water infrastructure.
reforms.component_name: On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances agricultural resilience to drought, thereby supporting productivity and sustainability in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was launched with allocated funding for drought preparedness.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme represents a significant investment in agricultural infrastructure, aimed at enhancing resilience to climate change impacts.
reforms.source_quote: The On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme was launched in December 2018 and includes AUD 100 million in funding for farmers to improve drought preparedness.

reforms.description: The Future Drought Fund provides AUD 100 million each year to help farmers and communities build drought resilience, focusing on improving climate information and planning capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supports long-term agricultural productivity and sustainability by enhancing resilience to climate variability.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund is operational and provides annual funding for drought resilience initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant commitment to enhancing agricultural resilience, with ongoing funding to support various initiatives.
reforms.source_quote: The Future Drought Fund provides AUD 100 million each year to help farmers and communities build drought resilience.

reforms.description: The Australian government is developing a National Adaptation Plan to address climate change impacts specifically in the energy sector, which will identify priorities and coordinate further action.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing climate change impacts, the plan aims to enhance the resilience of the energy sector, potentially leading to more stable energy supply and improved economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A National Adaptation Plan, announced in the 2023-24 Budget, is currently under development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant step towards addressing climate change in the energy sector, which is crucial for Australia's transition to net zero.
reforms.source_quote: A National Adaptation Plan, announced in the 2023-24 Budget, is currently under development.
